vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Carries wing spar loads into fuselage spine
Electrical/data flow between avionics bay and external systems
Heat dissipation from sun exposure to ambient air
Rotation of nacelle tilt mechanism around bearing seats
Pressure differential across belly camera window

integration_opportunities.description: Access for maintenance and repair of avionics bay components
Holes and cutouts for wiring harnesses and data cables
Mounting points for additional sensors and equipment

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for overhangs during FDM printing
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit, CMM for dimensional accuracy

verification_focus.value: All connectors mate correctly and pass continuity test
Temperature rise under simulated sunlight does not exceed 50°C
All moving parts rotate freely within specified clearance

generated_parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

requirements: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Structures - Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters)
Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible
Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable
Output remains educational mockup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details
Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers

verify.rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value

verify.value: All required evidence_features are present in part_tree/geometry_ops
verify.rule: Each required evidence feature must be represented in the geometry

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal naming and specifications
verify.rule: Interfaces must have reciprocal naming and specifications between adjacent modules

verify.value: Visible locking/preload evidence on critical joints
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence

verify.value: All required fields are present and correctly filled
verify.rule: The schema must be complete and all required fields must be filled

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to sun exposure
risk.mit: Implement cooling channels and heat sinks within the wing structure

risk.desc: Complexity in assembly due to multiple interfaces
risk.mit: Ensure clear assembly order hints and detailed instructions

risk.desc: Risk of overloading wing spar during nacelle tilt
risk.mit: Conduct thorough FEA analysis to ensure structural integrity

risk.desc: Difficulty in accessing service ports for maintenance
risk.mit: Design larger and more accessible service access panels

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during bolted connections
risk.mit: Use precision-machined bolts and ensure proper alignment tools

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

physics_paths.description: Loads from the nacelle are transferred through the wing spar into the fuselage spine.
Service access for motor and servos through dedicated access panels.
Thermal management through heat sinks and cooling channels within the wing structure.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate wing spar with fuselage spine for load transfer.
Ensure service access for motor and servos through dedicated access panels.
Implement cooling channels within the wing structure for thermal management.

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Dimensional checks, visual inspection, non-destructive testing

verification_focus.description: Verify the mass of the wing is within the budgeted range.
Check the structural integrity of the wing spar and its connections.
Ensure all service access points are accessible and unobstructed.
